question_text: Let X be a random variable having the probability density function $ f ( x ) = \left\{ { \begin{array} { l l } { \frac { 3 } { 1 3 } ( 1 - x ) ( 9 - x ) , } & { 0 < x < 1 , } \\ { 0 , } & { \mathrm { o t h e r w i s e } . } \end{array} } \right. $ Then $ { \frac { 4 } { 3 } } E [ ( X ^ { 2 } - 1 5 X + 2 7 ) ] { \mathrm { ~ e q u a l s } } { \mathrm { ~ } } _ { \mathrm { -- e q u a l ~ o f f ~ t o ~ } } 2 { \mathrm { ~ d e c i m a l ~ p l a c e s } } ) . $
solution:
First, expand the expression $X ^ { 2 } - 1 5 X + 2 7$ , and then compute the expected value using the given probability density function.

 $ E [ X ^ { 2 } - 1 5 X + 2 7 ] = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } ( x ^ { 2 } - 1 5 x + 2 7 ) \cdot { \frac { 3 } { 1 3 } } ( 1 - x ) ( 9 - x ) d x . $ 

Evaluating this integral gives approximately 8.60.

Thus,

 $ { \frac { 4 } { 3 } } E [ ( X ^ { 2 } - 1 5 X + 2 7 ) ] = { \frac { 4 } { 3 } } \times 8 . 6 0 = 8 . 7 5 . $ 

Thus, the value is 8.75.
Quick Tip

To solve such integrals, expand the polynomial terms first and then integrate each term with respect to the given probability density function.
